assumptions: expects a 2D array with at least two aligned channels
works best when window and rank reflect the dominant temporal structure
MSSA should be evaluated against residual diagnostics rather than used as a black box
metadata.name: MSSA
not_recommended_for: single-series workflows where a univariate flagship method is sufficient
very short series that cannot support a sensible window length

package_links.note: SSA-focused package; useful comparison point for SSA-family workflows.
package_links.title: SSALib

parameter_docs.description: Shared embedding window length for aligned channels.
parameter_docs.name: window

parameter_docs.description: Number of shared elementary components to retain.
parameter_docs.name: rank

parameter_docs.description: Dominant shared period used by automatic grouping.
parameter_docs.name: primary_period

parameter_docs.description: Sampling frequency used by frequency-based grouping.
parameter_docs.name: fs

parameter_docs.description: Explicit component indexes assigned to trend.
parameter_docs.name: trend_components

parameter_docs.description: Explicit component indexes assigned to season.
parameter_docs.name: season_components

references.note: Primary SSA/MSSA reference used for the multivariate extension.
references.title: Golyandina and Zhigljavsky (2020), Singular Spectrum Analysis for Time Series

metadata.summary: Multivariate SSA for shared-structure decomposition across channels.
typical_failure_modes: window or rank too small for the shared structure

recommendations.summary: Multivariate SSA for shared-structure decomposition across channels.

assumptions: treats each channel independently under one shared method surface
works best when one seasonal period or block structure is reasonably stable
STDR should be evaluated against residual diagnostics rather than used as a black box
metadata.name: STDR
not_recommended_for: problems that require one shared latent model across channels
series where the dominant period is unknown and cannot be inferred reliably

parameter_docs.description: Optional search horizon when period inference is enabled.
parameter_docs.name: max_period_search

parameter_docs.description: Small numerical guard for robust dispersion calculations.
parameter_docs.name: eps

references.note: Primary reference for STD and the robust seasonal-trend-dispersion family.
references.title: Dudek (2022), STD: A Seasonal-Trend-Dispersion Decomposition of Time Series

metadata.summary: Robust seasonal-trend decomposition for noisier periodic signals.
typical_failure_modes: heavy structural breaks that violate shared seasonal assumptions

recommendations.summary: Robust seasonal-trend decomposition for noisier periodic signals.
